headline: Is Netflix (NFLX) Pricing In Too Much Growth After Its Strong Multi Year Run
summary: If you have been wondering whether Netflix is fairly priced or if there is still value left in the share price, this breakdown will help you see what the market might be paying for today. At a last close of US$99.02, Netflix has returns of 2.0% over 7 days, 20.5% over 30 days, 8.8% year to date, 14.3% over 1 year, very large returns over 3 years and 90.3% over 5 years, which raises fresh questions about what is already built into the price. Recently, investors have been paying close...

headline: Trump Was Quietly Loading Up On Netflix Bonds — While Talking Down Its Warner Bid
summary: President Donald Trump openly discussed the potential merger between Netflix Inc and Warner Bros. Discovery for months. It turns out his personal investment portfolio held positions in Netflix bonds, which may have been at risk had the streamer won its bid for Warner Bros. Trump Buys Netflix Bonds Benzinga previously reported that Trump bought Netflix and Warner Bros. headline: Free of Warner Bros., Netflix Is a Growth Stock Once Again
summary: Netflix’s (NASDAQ:NFLX) ambitious push to acquire key assets from Warner Bros. Discovery (NASDAQ:WBD) in late 2025 quickly turned sour. Investors feared the roughly $83 billion cash deal — priced at $27.75 per share — would saddle the streamer with massive new debt, crimping margins and diverting capital from its core streaming business. Shares plunged as ... Free of Warner Bros., Netflix Is a Growth Stock Once Again

headline: H World, Netflix and JD.com Are Getting Fresh Analyst Coverage Across Global Consumer Markets
summary: Across three distinct global consumer markets, fresh analyst coverage is drawing a clear map of where institutional conviction sits heading into mid-2026. The signals are mixed: one bullish upgrade, one cautious resumption of coverage, and one target cut — each grounded in a specific thesis about how profitability will evolve. headline: Co-Founder Reed Hastings Just Dumped $40 Million in Netflix Stock. Should You Ditch NFLX Too?
summary: With 325 million paid members, operating income and free cash flow rising, and a 47-analyst "Moderate Buy" consensus plus average targets around $125-$130, implying mid‑teens upside from about $99, the setup favors holding or buying dips rather than ditching NFLX solely because its co‑founder trimmed a sliver of his stake.

raw.headline: Co-Founder Reed Hastings Just Dumped $40 Million in Netflix Stock. Should You Ditch NFLX Too?
raw.summary: With 325 million paid members, operating income and free cash flow rising, and a 47-analyst "Moderate Buy" consensus plus average targets around $125-$130, implying mid‑teens upside from about $99, the setup favors holding or buying dips rather than ditching NFLX solely because its co‑founder trimmed a sliver of his stake.

headline: Netflix Goes From M&A Loser to Market Winner Without Warner Deal
summary: The streaming giant emerged as the favorite to buy Warner in early December and agreed to a $72 billion acquisition on Dec. 5 that eventually increased to $83 billion.  Netflix shares immediately fell, as investors worried that the deal would distract the company from its core business and Neflix didn’t need the deal for growth.  Along the way, Paramount Skydance Corp. surfaced as another suitor for Warner and refused to drop its bid even after Warner said it preferred Netflix.

raw.headline: Netflix Goes From M&A Loser to Market Winner Without Warner Deal

Along the way, Paramount Skydance Corp. surfaced as another suitor for Warner and refused to drop its bid even after Warner said it preferred Netflix.
